free_text: Natural Sb was vacuum deposited onto Kapton (25um)
composed with average Sb layer of 1 mg/cm2. Ti foils,
5 um, were inserted in the stack for monitoring
Cross sections were measured by using charged
Activity measurement of the reaction products
was done by Gamma-spectrometry based on HPGe detectors.
Stacked foils targets was irradiated with
alpha-particle beam of 50.42 MeV, and 50 nA for 2 h.
Energy degradation was calculated by using the
polynomial approximation of Andersen ad Ziegler.

The uncertainty on the median energy in the
foils includes the cumulative effects of uncertainty of
incident beam energy, uncertainty of energy loss of
each foil due to thickness variation and straggling
effects. Uncertainty of the primari incident beam
energy was +- 0.2MeV which increased up to +- 0.9 MeV
in the last Ni foil of the stack.
The uncertainty on the cross section values
are estimated in the standard way as the square root
of the quadratic summation of the independently
contributing parameters. Uncertainty of time parameters
